freshness_caveat: Coal data is 144 days old (as of 26 Dec 2025) and Clean Dark is 139 days old (as of 31 Dec 2025); both snapshots predate the current metrics window and may not reflect current global spot prices. EU Storage, TTF, EUA, and power (DE/GB) are current as of 17–19 May 2026.
risk_flags: Coal data is 144 days old (as of 26 Dec 2025); 8th-percentile signal may not reflect current global spot.
EU storage at 36.56% (13.5 pp below 5-yr seasonal) with only 2 days of data freshness; refill pace into summer will determine H2 power curve steepness.
US industrial gas demand at record highs through 2027 tightens LNG export pool to Europe; TTF upside capped by lower netback incentive.
Far-right political opposition to renewable permitting in Europe risks multi-month buildout delays; DA thermal margins could widen further if wind/solar capacity slips.

base.summary: Gas-to-coal inversion persists; storage deficit sustains elevated thermal merit; LNG arb muted by US demand and sanctions.

downside.summary: US sanctions waiver extended and Hormuz tensions ease; crude-gas linkage weakens; EU storage refill accelerates; thermal call retreats.

upside.summary: Hormuz escalation or Strait closure risk spikes; Brent rallies, lifting TTF netback and gas-to-coal spread wider; storage refill pace disappoints.

themes: Gas-to-coal inversion extreme; thermal merit order compressed
EU storage deficit and LNG supply risk from US industrial demand
Renewable permitting delays tighten Cal+1 generation capacity
Hormuz/sanctions geopolitics cap LNG netback and crude support
top_drivers: Clean Spark at 95th percentile reflects extreme gas-to-coal inversion; merit order heavily biased toward thermal dispatch.
EU storage 13.5 pp below seasonal average with slow refill pace into summer tightens H2 power fundamentals and widens Cal+1 curve.
US industrial gas demand surge and Russian oil sanctions waiver suppress LNG export availability and crude-gas linkage; TTF upside capped.
Far-right political opposition to renewable permitting delays EU wind/solar buildout; tightens generation capacity and sustains thermal margins.
top_takeaway: Clean Spark at 95th percentile on gas-to-coal inversion; EU storage 13.5 pp below seasonal average tightens H2 power risk despite stale coal data and geopolitical overhang on LNG.

watchlist.why: June deadline renewal; Strait closure risk affects Brent-to-TTF crude-gas link and LNG netback arbitrage to NW Europe.
If granted, unlocks state capex for gas storage and LNG terminals; reduces H2 supply-side risk and supports Cal+1 power curve.
Political delays to wind/solar buildout tighten generation capacity and widen thermal dispatch margins into 2027; DA and curve shape vulnerable.

watchlist.metric: US LNG export volumes and industrial elasticity
watchlist.why: Record high industrial demand reduces Europe-bound LNG; lower netback support caps TTF rally and shifts global supply competition.

watchlist_dated.name: US Russian oil sanctions waiver renewal
watchlist_dated.why: Determines Brent-to-TTF crude pass-through and LNG netback arbitrage floor for NW Europe through H2.
